ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Singapore economic rice with three vegetable dishes; 'cooked rice dish' (FOODON_00002694) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Singapore cai fan / economic rice; A00LC (rice-based mixed dish) is the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific hawker dish preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker-centre plate of economic rice with three vegetable dishes weighs approximately 350 g, comprising roughly 200 g steamed white rice and 150 g of three mixed vegetable portions.

nutrient_source.title: Economic Rice (3 Vegetables) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Economic Rice (3 Vegetables), CR ID F05100047. All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice, steamed, Asian-style
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 72 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for steamed white rice (Asian-style), which forms the dominant carbohydrate component of this dish. The three vegetable dishes contribute minimal additional carbohydrate and are not expected to materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 49.14 g carbohydrate in 350 g serving) / 100 ≈ 35.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 197 mg/100 g; a 350 g serving delivers approximately 690 mg sodium, which is 30% of the 2,300 mg daily limit and warrants monitoring.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced sauce and minimally salted vegetable preparations to keep per-serving sodium below 600 mg, and pair this meal with potassium-rich foods to support blood pressure control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (72) refined white rice base with 14.04 g carbohydrate per 100 g and a glycemic load of approximately 35 per serving creates a significant postprandial glucose challenge.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce the rice portion to 100–150 g, increase the vegetable proportion, and consider adding a lean protein (e.g. tofu or egg) to attenuate the glycaemic response of this high-GI meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat at 3.52 g/100 g is moderate; saturated fat breakdown is not available from the HPB source, and sodium at 197 mg/100 g (690 mg per serving) requires attention for cardiovascular risk management.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should choose vegetable dishes prepared with minimal oil and request less sauce to limit sodium and saturated fat intake, while benefiting from the dietary fibre (1.49 g/100 g) and antioxidants provided by the three vegetable dishes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Three vegetable dishes provide antioxidants (vitamin C, carotenoids) and dietary fibre supporting anti-inflammatory dietary patterns.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should select colourful vegetables (e.g. carrots, leafy greens, broccoli) and request minimal oil in stir-frying to maximise anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Economic Rice (3 Vegetables), known locally as 'cai fan' (菜饭 / 經濟飯), is a quintessential Singaporean hawker-centre staple consisting of steamed white rice served alongside three self-selected vegetable dishes. The meal is assembled at open-display hawker stalls where diners choose from a rotating selection of stir-fried, braised, or blanched vegetables such as kai lan, long beans, cabbage, tofu, spinach, and bean sprouts. The rice base is soft and mildly flavoured, while the vegetable dishes vary in taste from savoury and garlicky to lightly sweet or umami-rich depending on the preparation. Per 100 g, the dish provides 99 kcal, 2.31 g protein, 3.52 g fat, 14.04 g carbohydrate, 1.49 g dietary fibre, and 197 mg sodium, as reported by the HPB Singapore Foodo database. The glycemic index is high (GI 72) due to the refined white rice base, making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. The three vegetable dishes contribute meaningful dietary fibre, antioxidants, and micronutrients including calcium and iron. This meal is vegetarian, vegan, halal, and gluten-free by default, though individual vegetable preparations may vary. It is a common working-lunch choice across Singapore's hawker centres and coffee shops, valued for its affordability, variety, and plant-forward nutritional profile.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F05100047) with a reported confidence of 0.95. This is the primary approved source for Singaporean hawker dishes. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using steamed white rice as the analogous food, which is the dominant carbohydrate source in this dish.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite hawker dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F05100047 as of 2026-04-16. GI value of 72 is an analogous estimate based on steamed white rice (Asian-style) from the University of Sydney GI Database, as no direct GI measurement for this specific composite dish was found. Glycemic load per serving calculated using a 350 g serving size: GL = (72 × 49.14 g carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 35.4. Dietary flags (vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free) reflect the default three-vegetable preparation; individual stall preparations may vary. The input record listed 'rheumatoid_arthritis' as a health suitability condition; this has been retained in additional_conditions. The input condition key 'diabetes' has been updated to 'type2_diabetes' per Veritas naming rules.
